rulings.Case Title: MAYRA LOYA ET AL VS. RAYMOND GRINSELL, TRUSTEE OF THE EQUITY GROWTH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT To The Complaint Of Carlos Loya, Or, In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 2, 2022 line 1. DEFENDANT RAYMOND GRINSELL, TRUSTEE OF THE EQUITY GROWTH, FOUNDERS REALTY, INC MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT To The Complaint Of Carlos Loya, Or, In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication is DENIED. Moving party failed to shift its burden and dispose of any of the causes of action in their entirety, i.e. motion addresses one incident of fire, characterized as "the crux" of all claims. Motion for Summary Judgment is framed by the pleadings and complaint in this action is not based solely on one incident of fire. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MAREN CHRISTENSEN VS. ABC COMMERICAL REALTY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion Of Greentree Property Management, Inc. And Veritas Investments, Inc. For Good Faith Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 2, 2022 line 2. DEFENDANT GREENTREE PROPERTY MANAGEMENT, INC., VERITAS INVESTMENTS, INC. Motion Of Greentree Property Management, Inc. And Veritas Investments, Inc. For Good Faith Settlement is DENIED. Insufficient information provided for the Court to make a determination of proportionate lability for plaintiff's alleged injury. As such, no determination may be made at this time as to the rough approximation of plaintiff's total recovery and the settlor's proportionate liability. The Court declines moving party's invitation to "consult[aion] with others" (Reply 2:18-19) to make this determination. The Court notes that it was not able to identify the font and style of the opposition papers; and that the font and style does not appear to comply with CRC 2.104 and 2.105, i.e. essentially equivalent to Courier, Times New Roman or Arial in size 12 points. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
